sessions.intent: Implement crypto module with pluggable provider architecture (Engine.Providers)

before.assumptions: Test stubs and plan already exist from test-designer. Identity module patterns are the reference for handler structure.
before.risk: Low — self-contained module with no dependencies on existing modules

sessions.plan: Implement ICryptoProvider interface, DefaultProvider (keccak256/sha256), HashedData type, hash handler, verify handler. Fill in test stubs. Skip bcrypt tests. Add Engine.Providers as generic pluggable implementation registry.

actions.context: Core crypto module: interface, default provider, types, hash and verify action handlers. Handlers are thin — resolve provider, serialize data, delegate to provider.
Engine.Providers — type-keyed ConcurrentDictionary<Type, object> for pluggable module implementations.
Replaced 15 stub tests with real implementations. Added [Skip] to 4 bcrypt tests. All 26 active tests pass.
Updated Foundation Checklist item #4 to DONE.

after.notes: Design evolved during session: plan called for DataSource provider resolution, but DataSource serializes to JSON. Discussed with Ingi -> MemoryStack -> then designed Engine.Providers as the proper generic solution for pluggable module implementations.

sessions.intent: Code analysis v1: OBP compliance, simplification, readability, behavioral reasoning for crypto module

before.assumptions: Crypto module is self-contained. Identity module changes (SensitivePropertyFilter, Actor.Identity, IdentityData) are from the identity branch and already reviewed there.
before.risk: Low — reviewing new code, not modifying

sessions.plan: 5-pass analysis of crypto module files (hash.cs, verify.cs, types.cs, ICryptoProvider.cs, DefaultProvider.cs) + Engine.Providers + identity-related changes. Focus on OBP compliance, simplification opportunities, behavioral reasoning (esp. algorithm validation paths), and deletion tests.

actions.context: 5-pass analysis: OBP, simplification, readability, behavioral reasoning, deletion test. 4 findings (1 medium, 3 low). Main: DefaultProvider.Verify duplicates algorithm validation.

after.health: NEEDS WORK — 1 medium finding (redundant algorithm validation), 3 low. MISSED: providers throwing exceptions instead of returning Data.
after.notes: Architecture is clean. OBP-compliant throughout. Engine.Providers well-designed. Ingi flagged critical miss: ICryptoProvider threw exceptions instead of returning Data.

sessions.intent: Code analysis v2: re-review after coder fixes + Ingi feedback on provider exceptions

before.assumptions: Coder fixed all v1 findings + provider exception issue. Need to verify fixes don't introduce new problems.

sessions.plan: Re-review 4 changed files (ICryptoProvider, DefaultProvider, hash.cs, verify.cs) + 3 test files. Verify provider returns Data, handlers check .Success, tests assert error keys.

actions.context: All findings resolved. Providers return Data, no exceptions in domain code, tests verify error propagation.

after.health: PASS — all findings resolved, crypto module clean
after.notes: Saved learning: 'behavior methods never throw' applies to ALL domain code (providers, not just handlers). Updated memory.

sessions.intent: Test quality analysis for crypto module — validate test honesty and coverage

before.assumptions: Coder v1 complete, codeanalyzer v2 passed. Tests should be in good shape. PLang tests not buildable yet.
before.risk: Low — reviewing tests, not modifying code

sessions.plan: Run C# tests, run coverage, apply deletion test to all crypto source files, check assertion quality, verify review-driven code has tests, assess PLang test status, write report.

actions.context: Full test quality analysis. 1675 pass, 0 fail, 4 skipped. 5 minor findings: untested Providers methods, two weak assertions, PLang tests deferred, HashedData.ToString() untested. No false greens.

after.health: APPROVED — test suite is honest, all critical paths covered
after.notes: Coverage tool doesn't instrument source-generator partial classes. Tests exercise the code but coverage numbers are unavailable for crypto files specifically.
